text:
Eleanor had lived in that house alone for years. It was old, with many secrets. One afternoon, while baking bread, she heard a slight creak upstairs. Her curiosity piqued; she tiptoed upstairs.

She peeked through one of the unlocked doors. There was Hank, the cat, stretched lazily on his favorite cushion. Behind him, on a cluttered desk, was a map. Eleanor picked it up—they were plotting explorations through the dense forest behind their farmhouse, which Hank dearly loved.

With a chuckle, Eleanor shook her head. Hank was a lover of mysteries far more exciting than her own detective work. But later that night, as Eleanor lay asleep in her bed, a shadowy figure slipped into her room. It wasn't Hank; it was Jim—a neighbor.

They began exchanging stories of their exploits; Eleanor spoke about her mysteries, and Jim, who loved a good yarn, told her about his secret life mapping haunted locations. That's when
